citations: Heckman 1993 labor supply review

runs.reasoningSummary: My prior is that the intensive-margin uncompensated hours elasticity for employed prime-age workers is small but positive: substitution effects are modest and partly offset by income effects, with most estimates for men near zero and for women somewhat higher. runs.reasoningSummary: For already-working prime-age workers, I expect annual hours to respond only modestly to net-of-tax wage changes once participation is held fixed. Income effects can offset substitution effects, so the uncompensated intensive-margin elasticity is likely near zero but slightly positive on average.
